question: What are the total quantities of the items ordered by customer 101660 on 10/5/1995?
evidence: total quantity refers to sum(l_quantity); customer 101660 refers to o_custkey = 101660; on 10/5/1995 refers to o_orderdate = '1995-10-05'
SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.l_quantity) FROM orders AS T1 INNER JOIN lineitem AS T2 ON T1.o_orderkey = T2.l_orderkey WHERE T1.o_orderdate = '1995-10-05' AND T1.o_custkey = 101660

question: Identify the names and positions of the players who have committed the most rule violations across all seasons.
evidence: name of the player refers to PlayerName; position of the player refers to position_info; committed the most rule violations refers to MAX(SUM(PIM));
SQL: SELECT p.`PlayerName`, p.`position_info` FROM `PlayerInfo` p JOIN (SELECT `ELITEID`, SUM(`PIM`) AS total_pim FROM `SeasonStatus` GROUP BY `ELITEID` HAVING total_pim = ( SELECT MAX(total_pim) FROM ( SELECT `ELITEID`, SUM(`PIM`) AS total_pim FROM `SeasonStatus` GROUP BY `ELITEID` ) AS PIMTotals )) s ON p.`ELITEID` = s.`ELITEID`
original_question: Identify the name and position of the player who has committed the most rule violations.
original_evidence: name of the player refers to PlayerName; position of the player refers to position_info; committed the most rule violations refers to MAX(PIM);
original_SQL: SELECT T2.PlayerName, T2.position_info FROM SeasonStatus AS T1 INNER JOIN PlayerInfo AS T2 ON T1.ELITEID = T2.ELITEID WHERE T1.PIM = ( SELECT MAX(PIM) FROM SeasonStatus )

question: How many players who were drafted by the Toronto Maple Leafs have played over 300 games in their first 7 years of the NHL career?
evidence: drafted by the Toronto Maple Leafs refers to overallby = 'Toronto Maple Leafs'; played over 300 games in their first 7 years of the NHL career refers to sum_7yr_GP > 300;
SQL: SELECT COUNT(ELITEID) FROM PlayerInfo WHERE overallby = 'Toronto Maple Leafs' AND sum_7yr_GP > 300

question: Which teams have the most Swedish?
evidence: Swedish refers to nation = 'Sweden'; team with the most Swedish refers to MAX(TEAM WHERE nation = 'Sweden');
SQL: SELECT T.TEAM FROM ( SELECT T2.TEAM, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) FROM PlayerInfo AS T1 INNER JOIN SeasonStatus AS T2 ON T1.ELITEID = T2.ELITEID WHERE T1.nation = 'Sweden' GROUP BY T2.TEAM HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) FROM PlayerInfo AS T1 INNER JOIN SeasonStatus AS T2 ON T1.ELITEID = T2.ELITEID WHERE T1.nation = 'Sweden' GROUP BY T2.TEAM ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) DESC LIMIT 1)  ) AS T
original_question: Which team has the most Swedish?
original_SQL: SELECT T.TEAM FROM ( SELECT T2.TEAM, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) FROM PlayerInfo AS T1 INNER JOIN SeasonStatus AS T2 ON T1.ELITEID = T2.ELITEID WHERE T1.nation = 'Sweden' GROUP BY T2.TEAM ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ELITEID) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T

question: List the names of the products whereby the standard cost is at least $80 more than previous standard cost in history.
evidence: SUBTRACT(product.StandardCost, CostHistory.StandardCost)>=80
SQL: SELECT T1.Name FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductCostHistory AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T1.StandardCost - T2.StandardCost >= 80 GROUP BY T1.Name
original_question: List the products whereby the standard cost is $80 more than previous standard cost in history.
original_evidence: SUBTRACT(product.StandardCost, CostHistory.StandardCost)>80
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Name FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductCostHistory AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T1.StandardCost - T2.StandardCost > 80 GROUP BY T1.Name

question: List the names and calculate their profit for products with the highest rating in review.
evidence: Profit = SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost); the highest rating in review refers to MAX(Rating)
SQL: SELECT p.`Name`, p.`ListPrice` - p.`StandardCost` AS profit FROM   `Product` p JOIN   `ProductReview` pr ON p.`ProductID` = pr.`ProductID` WHERE  pr.`Rating` = (SELECT MAX(`Rating`) FROM `ProductReview`)
original_question: List the name and calculate its profit for product with the highest rating in review.
original_evidence: Profit = SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost); the highest rating in review refers to Rating = 5
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Name, T1.ListPrice - T1.StandardCost FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductReview AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID ORDER BY T2.Rating DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which currency pairs' average exchange rates for the day are the highest?
evidence: currency pair refers to FromCurrencyCode/ToCurrencyCode
SQL: SELECT FromCurrencyCode, ToCurrencyCode FROM CurrencyRate WHERE AverageRate = (SELECT MAX(AverageRate) FROM CurrencyRate);
original_question: Which currency pair's average exchange rate for the day is the highest?
original_SQL: SELECT FromCurrencyCode, ToCurrencyCode FROM CurrencyRate ORDER BY AverageRate DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the employees who are married, how many of them have a western name style?
evidence: married refers to MaritalStatus = 'M'; western name style refers to NameStyle = '0';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.NameStyle = 0 AND T1.MaritalStatus = 'M'

question: Among the employees who have more than 10 hours of sick leave, how many of them wish to receive e-mail promotions?
evidence: Contact does wish to receive e-mail promotions refers to EmailPromotion = (1,2); more than 10 hours of sick leave refer to SickLeaveHours >10;
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `Employee` AS e JOIN `Person`  AS p ON e.`BusinessEntityID` = p.`BusinessEntityID` WHERE e.`SickLeaveHours` > 10 AND p.`EmailPromotion` IN (1,2)
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.EmailPromotion = 1 AND T1.SickLeaveHours > 10

question: Please give the additional contact information of the oldest employee with the job position of sales person.
evidence: Sales person refers to PersonType = 'SP'; oldest employee refers to Min(BirthDate);
SQL: SELECT T2.AdditionalContactInfo FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE PersonType = 'SP' AND T1.BirthDate = (SELECT MIN(BirthDate) FROM Employee WHERE PersonType = 'SP');
original_question: Please give the additional contact information of the oldest employee with the jod position of sales person.
original_evidence: Sales person refers to PersonType = 'SP'; oldest employee refers to Max (Subtract((now())-BirthDate));
original_SQL: SELECT T2.AdditionalContactInfo FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE PersonType = 'SP' ORDER BY T1.BirthDate ASC LIMIT 1

question: Please list the credit card IDs of the employees who work as store contact.
evidence: store contact refers to PersonType = 'SC';
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT pc.`CreditCardID` FROM `Person` p JOIN `Employee` e ON p.`BusinessEntityID` = e.`BusinessEntityID` JOIN `PersonCreditCard` pc ON p.`BusinessEntityID` = pc.`BusinessEntityID` WHERE p.`PersonType` = 'SC'
original_SQL: SELECT T2.CreditCardID FROM Person AS T1 INNER JOIN PersonCreditCard AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T1.PersonType = 'SC'

question: What is the credit rating of the company whose average lead time is 16 days for a standard price of 18.9900 and whose last receipt date is August 27, 2011?
evidence: last receipt date is August 17, 2011 refers to LastReceiptDate> = '2011-08-17 00:00:00' and LastReceiptDate < '2011-08-18 00:00:00';
SQL: SELECT T2.CreditRating FROM ProductVendor AS T1 INNER JOIN Vendor AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T1.StandardPrice = 18.9900 AND T1.AverageLeadTime = 16 AND STRFTIME('%Y-%m-%d', T1.LastReceiptDate) = '2011-08-27'

question: How many products are there if we add all those located in the Subassembly category?
evidence: located in the Subassembly category refers to Name = 'Subassembly'
SQL: SELECT SUM(`Quantity`) AS TotalItems FROM `ProductInventory` WHERE `LocationID` = (SELECT `LocationID` FROM `Location` WHERE `Name` = 'Subassembly')
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.LocationID) FROM Location AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductInventory AS T2 USING (LocationID) WHERE T1.Name = 'Subassembly'

question: What percentage of product-model description rows are stored in Thai?
evidence: percentage = DIVIDE(Culture.Name = 'Thai', count(ALL Culture.Name))*100%
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Name = 'Thai' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.CultureID) FROM Culture AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductModelProductDescriptionCulture AS T2 ON T1.CultureID = T2.CultureID
original_question: What percentage of the AdventureWorks data is in Thai?

question: What is the size of the thumbnail photo of product id No. 332 in bytes?
SQL: SELECT length(pp.`ThumbNailPhoto`) AS PhotoSizeBytes FROM `ProductProductPhoto` ppp JOIN `ProductPhoto` pp ON pp.`ProductPhotoID` = ppp.`ProductPhotoID` WHERE ppp.`ProductID` = 332
original_question: What is the size of the photo of product id No.1?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.ThumbNailPhoto FROM ProductPhoto AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductProductPhoto AS T2 ON T1.ProductPhotoID = T2.ProductPhotoID WHERE T2.ProductID = 1

question: For person id No.2054, is his/her vendor still active? Output the activeflag.
evidence: ActiveFlag = 1 means 'Vendor no longer used'; ActiveFlag = 2 means 'Vendor is actively used
SQL: SELECT T1.ActiveFlag FROM Vendor AS T1 INNER JOIN BusinessEntityContact AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.PersonID = 2054
original_question: For person id No.2054, is his/her vendor still active?

question: What's Lynn N Tsoflias's job title?
SQL: SELECT T2.JobTitle FROM Person AS T1 INNER JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T1.FirstName = 'Lynn' AND T1.MiddleName = 'N' AND T1.LastName = 'Tsoflias'

question: Which product ID do not have any work order ID?
evidence: Do not have any work order ID means WorkOrderID is null
SQL: SELECT ProductID FROM Product WHERE ProductID NOT IN ( SELECT T1.ProductID FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN WorkOrder AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID )

question: How many people with the name Alex are single and occupying organization level of 1?
evidence: Alex refers to FirstName = 'Alex'; single refers to MaritalStatus = 'S'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.FirstName = 'Alex' AND T1.MaritalStatus = 'S' AND T1.OrganizationLevel = 1

question: Among the sales people who achieved projected sales quota 2013, is there any person from territory ID 1? If yes, state the business entity ID.
evidence: projected sales quota refers to SalesQuota; projected sales quota in 2013 refers to year(QuotaDate) = 2013;
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT sp.BusinessEntityID FROM SalesPerson AS sp JOIN SalesPersonQuotaHistory AS sq ON sp.BusinessEntityID = sq.BusinessEntityID WHERE sp.TerritoryID = 1 AND STRFTIME('%Y', sq.QuotaDate) = '2013' AND sp.Bonus <> 0
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.BusinessEntityID FROM SalesPerson AS T1 INNER JOIN SalesPersonQuotaHistory AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T1.TerritoryID = 1 AND STRFTIME('%Y', QuotaDate) = '2013'

question: Among the employees who wish to receive e-mail promotion from AdventureWorks, how many percent of them are female?
evidence: female refers to Gender = 'F'; employee who wish to receive email promotion refers to EmailPromotion = 1; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(Gender = 'F')), (sum(Gender = 'F' or Gender = 'M'))) as percentage;
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN e.Gender = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS FemalePercent FROM Employee e JOIN Person p ON e.BusinessEntityID = p.BusinessEntityID WHERE p.EmailPromotion IN (1, 2)
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Gender = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.EmailPromotion = 1

question: How many types of credit cards are there and how many are vista?
evidence: type of credit card refers to CardType; CardType = 'Vista';
SQL: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT `CardType`) FROM `CreditCard`) AS total_card_types, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `CreditCard` WHERE `CardType` = 'Vista') AS vista_cards
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(CardNumber) FROM CreditCard WHERE CardType = 'vista'

question: How many married male employees were born before the year 1960?
evidence: married refers to MaritalStatus = 'M'; male refers to Gender = 'M'; BirthDate < = '1959-12-31';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee WHERE MaritalStatus = 'M' AND STRFTIME('%Y', BirthDate) < '1960' AND Gender = 'M'

question: Among the products from the mountain product line, how many of them are sold by over 2 vendors?
evidence: mountain product line refers to ProductLine = 'M'; sold by over 2 vendors refers to count(BusinessEntityID)>2
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ( SELECT p.`ProductID` FROM `Product` p JOIN `ProductVendor` pv ON p.`ProductID` = pv.`ProductID` WHERE p.`ProductLine` = 'M' GROUP BY p.`ProductID` HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pv.`BusinessEntityID`) > 2 ) AS sub
original_evidence: mountain product line refers to ProductLine = 'M'; sold by over 5 vendors refers to count(Name)>5
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T1.ProductLine = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductVendor AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID INNER JOIN Vendor AS T3 ON T2.BusinessEntityID = T3.BusinessEntityID GROUP BY T1.ProductID HAVING COUNT(T1.Name) > 2

question: Please list the names of the products that are out of stock and were purchased in house.
evidence: out of stock refers to OnOrderQty = 0 or OnOrderQty is null; purchased in house refers to MakeFlag = 0
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT p.Name FROM `Product` p WHERE p.`MakeFlag` = 0 AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM `ProductVendor` pv WHERE pv.`ProductID` = p.`ProductID` AND pv.`OnOrderQty` > 0)
original_question: Please list the products that are out of stock and purchased in house.
original_evidence: take more than 2 days to make refers to DaysToManufacture>2; out of stock refers to OnOrderQty = 0 or OnOrderQty is null; manufactured in house refers to MakeFlag = 1
original_SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM ProductVendor AS T1 INNER JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T2.MakeFlag = 0 AND (T1.OnOrderQty IS NULL OR T1.OnOrderQty = 0)

question: Name the oldest employees who are working on night shift. How old are the employees?
evidence: working on night shift refers to ShiftID = 3; oldest employee refers to min(BirthDate); age = year(now)-year(BirthDate)-(0 if birthday passed, 1 if not)
SQL: SELECT  p.`FirstName`, p.`LastName`, CAST( (strftime('%Y','now') - strftime('%Y',e.`BirthDate`)) - (strftime('%m-%d','now') < strftime('%m-%d',e.`BirthDate`)) AS INTEGER) AS Age FROM      EmployeeDepartmentHistory dh JOIN      Employee  e ON e.`BusinessEntityID` = dh.`BusinessEntityID` JOIN      Person    p ON p.`BusinessEntityID` = e.`BusinessEntityID` WHERE     dh.`ShiftID` = 3 AND     dh.`EndDate` IS NULL AND e.BirthDate = (SELECT MIN(e.BirthDate) FROM EmployeeDepartmentHistory dh JOIN      Employee  e ON e.`BusinessEntityID` = dh.`BusinessEntityID` JOIN      Person    p ON p.`BusinessEntityID` = e.`BusinessEntityID` WHERE     dh.`ShiftID` = 3 AND     dh.`EndDate` IS NULL) GROUP BY  e.`BusinessEntityID`
original_question: Name the oldest employee who is working on night shift. How old is the employee?
original_evidence: working on night shift refers to ShiftID = 3; oldest employee refers to min(BirthDate); age = 2022-year(BirthDate)+1
original_SQL: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName , STRFTIME('%Y', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) - STRFTIME('%Y', BirthDate) FROM Person AS T1 INNER JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID INNER JOIN EmployeeDepartmentHistory AS T3 ON T2.BusinessEntityID = T3.BusinessEntityID WHERE T3.ShiftId = 3 ORDER BY STRFTIME('%Y', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) - STRFTIME('%Y', BirthDate) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Please provide contact details of all Marketing Managers. State their first name, last name, and email address.
evidence: Marketing Manager is a job title
SQL: SELECT p.FirstName, p.LastName, ea.EmailAddress FROM   Person AS p JOIN   Employee AS e ON p.BusinessEntityID = e.BusinessEntityID LEFT JOIN EmailAddress AS ea ON p.BusinessEntityID = ea.BusinessEntityID WHERE  e.JobTitle = 'Marketing Manager'
original_question: Please provide contact details of all Marketing Managers. State their name and phone number.
original_SQL: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T2.PhoneNumber FROM Person AS T1 INNER JOIN PersonPhone AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID INNER JOIN Employee AS T3 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T3.BusinessEntityID WHERE T3.JobTitle = 'Marketing Manager'

question: What is the company's second highest salary per hour for employees who are paid monthly?
evidence: salary received monthly refers to PayFrequency = 1; highest salary per hour refers to Max(Rate);
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT Rate FROM EmployeePayHistory WHERE PayFrequency = 1 ORDER BY Rate DESC LIMIT 1, 1
original_SQL: SELECT Rate FROM EmployeePayHistory WHERE PayFrequency = 1 ORDER BY Rate DESC LIMIT 1, 1

question: How many products with the id "476" are stored in Metal Storage?
evidence: Metal Storage is name of location
SQL: SELECT T2.Quantity FROM Location AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductInventory AS T2 ON T1.LocationID = T2.LocationID WHERE T2.ProductID = 476 AND T1.Name = 'Metal Storage'

question: How many images have at least one object sample in the class of "man"?
evidence: have at least one object sample in the class of "man" refers to count(IMG_ID where OBJ_CLASS = 'man') > = 1
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T.IMG_ID) FROM ( SELECT T2.IMG_ID FROM OBJ_CLASSES AS T1 INNER JOIN IMG_OBJ AS T2 ON T1.OBJ_CLASS_ID = T2.OBJ_CLASS_ID WHERE T1.OBJ_CLASS = 'man' GROUP BY T2.IMG_ID ) T

question: How many object classes are there in the database?
evidence: object classes refers to OBJ_CLASS
SQL: SELECT COUNT(OBJ_CLASS_ID) FROM OBJ_CLASSES

question: What is the relationship between object sample no.12 and no.8 of image no.2345511?
evidence: relationship refers to PRED_CLASS; object sample no.12 and no.8 of image no.2345511 refers to IMG_ID = 2345511 AND OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 12 AND OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 8
SQL: SELECT T1.PRED_CLASS FROM PRED_CLASSES AS T1 INNER JOIN IMG_REL AS T2 ON T1.PRED_CLASS_ID = T2.PRED_CLASS_ID WHERE T2.IMG_ID = 2345511 AND T2.OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 12 AND T2.OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 8

question: List all the ids of the images that have a self-relation relationship.
evidence: ids of the images refers to IMG_ID; self-relations refers to OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT IMG_ID FROM IMG_REL WHERE OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID

question: Among the objects that have multiple relations, how many images whose captions for the prediction class ids are "on"?
evidence: objects that have multiple relations refers to OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID ! = OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID; captions for the prediction class ids are "on" refers to PRED_CLASS = 'on'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.IMG_ID) FROM IMG_REL AS T1 INNER JOIN PRED_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.PRED_CLASS_ID = T2.PRED_CLASS_ID WHERE T1.OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID != T1.OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID AND T2.PRED_CLASS = 'on';
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.PRED_CLASS_ID) FROM IMG_REL AS T1 INNER JOIN PRED_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.PRED_CLASS_ID = T2.PRED_CLASS_ID WHERE T1.OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID != T1.OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID AND T2.PRED_CLASS = 'on'

question: Define the onion's bounding box on image no. 285930.
evidence: bounding box refers to (X, Y, W, H); onion refers to OBJ_CLASS = 'onion'; image no.285930 refers to IMG_ID = 285930
SQL: SELECT T1.X, T1.Y, T1.W, T1.H FROM IMG_OBJ AS T1 INNER JOIN OBJ_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.OBJ_CLASS_ID = T2.OBJ_CLASS_ID WHERE T1.IMG_ID = 285930 AND T2.OBJ_CLASS = 'onion'

question: What colour is the van that can be spotted in image no. 1?
evidence: colour refers to ATT_CLASS; van refers to OBJ_CLASS = 'van'; image no. 1 refers to IMG_ID = 1
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT ac.`ATT_CLASS` FROM `IMG_OBJ`      AS io JOIN `OBJ_CLASSES`  AS oc ON oc.`OBJ_CLASS_ID` = io.`OBJ_CLASS_ID` JOIN `IMG_OBJ_ATT`  AS ia ON ia.`IMG_ID` = io.`IMG_ID` AND ia.`OBJ_SAMPLE_ID` = io.`OBJ_SAMPLE_ID` JOIN `ATT_CLASSES`  AS ac ON ac.`ATT_CLASS_ID` = ia.`ATT_CLASS_ID` WHERE oc.`OBJ_CLASS` = 'van' AND io.`IMG_ID`   = 1
original_SQL: SELECT T4.ATT_CLASS FROM IMG_OBJ AS T1 INNER JOIN OBJ_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.OBJ_CLASS_ID = T2.OBJ_CLASS_ID INNER JOIN IMG_OBJ_ATT AS T3 ON T1.IMG_ID = T3.IMG_ID INNER JOIN ATT_CLASSES AS T4 ON T3.ATT_CLASS_ID = T4.ATT_CLASS_ID WHERE T2.OBJ_CLASS = 'van' AND T1.IMG_ID = 1 GROUP BY T4.ATT_CLASS

question: What is the caption for the prediction class id 12?
evidence: caption for the prediction class id 12 refers to PRED_CLASS where PRED_CLASS_ID = 12;
SQL: SELECT PRED_CLASS FROM PRED_CLASSES WHERE PRED_CLASS_ID = 12

question: Which object class ids belong to the onion category?
evidence: onion category refers to OBJ_CLASS = 'onion';
SQL: SELECT OBJ_CLASS_ID FROM OBJ_CLASSES WHERE OBJ_CLASS = 'onion'
original_question: Which object classes belong to the onion category?

question: How many samples of animal objects are there in image no.660?
evidence: samples of animal objects refer to OBJ_SAMPLE_ID where OBJ_CLASS = 'animal'; image no.660 refers to IMG_ID = 660;
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.IMG_ID) FROM IMG_OBJ AS T1 INNER JOIN OBJ_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.OBJ_CLASS_ID = T2.OBJ_CLASS_ID WHERE T2.OBJ_CLASS = 'animal' AND T1.IMG_ID = 660

question: How many images have less than 15 object samples?
evidence: images refer to IMG_ID; less than 15 object samples refer to COUNT(OBJ_SAMPLE_ID) < 15;
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ( SELECT `IMG_ID` FROM `IMG_OBJ` GROUP BY `IMG_ID` HAVING COUNT(`OBJ_SAMPLE_ID`) < 15 ) AS few_objects
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(IMG_ID) FROM IMG_OBJ WHERE OBJ_SAMPLE_ID < 15

question: Among the players who won an award in the year 1983, how many of them play the position of goalie?
evidence: position of goalie refers to pos = 'G'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT `playerID`) FROM `AwardsPlayers` WHERE `year` = 1983 AND `pos` = 'G'
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(playerID) FROM AwardsPlayers WHERE pos = 'G' AND year = 1983

question: What are the weights of all the players who had the most Power Play Goals in the 21st century?
evidence: 21st century refers to year >2000; Power play goals refers to PPG
SQL: WITH player_totals AS ( SELECT s.playerID, SUM(s.PPG) AS totalPPG FROM Scoring s WHERE s.year > 2000 GROUP BY s.playerID ), max_val AS ( SELECT MAX(totalPPG) AS maxPPG FROM player_totals ) SELECT DISTINCT m.weight FROM player_totals p JOIN max_val mx ON p.totalPPG = mx.maxPPG JOIN Master m ON m.playerID = p.playerID
original_question: What's the weight of the player who had the most Power Play Goals in the 21st century?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.weight FROM Master AS T1 INNER JOIN Scoring AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T2.year > 2000 GROUP BY T1.playerID, T1.weight ORDER BY SUM(T2.PPG) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Who were the coaches for the teams which had the most bench minors penalty in 2003？
evidence: Coach of the team refers to firstName+lastName; 2003 refers to the year
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.firstName, T3.lastName FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName HAVING SUM(T1.BenchMinor) = (SELECT SUM(T1.BenchMinor) FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName ORDER BY SUM(T1.BenchMinor) DESC LIMIT 1);
original_question: Who was the coach for the team which had the most bench minors penalty in 2003？
original_evidence: Coach of the team refers to firstName+lastName; 2003 refers to the year
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.firstName, T3.lastName FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName ORDER BY SUM(T1.BenchMinor) DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are the power play percentages of the teams with the most number of loses?
evidence: Power play percentage refers to PPG/PCC, most number of loses refers to MAX(L)
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(PPG) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(PPC) FROM Teams GROUP BY tmID HAVING SUM(L) = (SELECT SUM(L) FROM Teams GROUP BY tmID ORDER BY SUM(L) DESC LIMIT 1);
original_question: What is the power play percentage of the team with the most number of loses?
original_evidence: Power play percentage refers to PPG, most number of loses refers to MAX(L)
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(PPG) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(PPC) FROM Teams GROUP BY tmID ORDER BY SUM(L) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the players whose short handed assists are greater or equal to 7, what are the final standings of the teams with the most number of assists? Indicate the years to which the most number of assists were achieved and the names of the teams.
evidence: short handed assists refers to SHA; SHA> = 7; final standing refers to rank; the final standing of the team with the most number of assists refers to max(A)
SQL: SELECT TM.`rank`, TM.`year`, TM.`name` FROM `Scoring` AS S JOIN `Teams`  AS TM ON S.`year` = TM.`year` AND S.`tmID` = TM.`tmID` WHERE CAST(S.`SHA` AS integer) >= 7 AND CAST(S.`A` AS integer) = (SELECT MAX(CAST(S2.`A` AS integer)) FROM `Scoring` AS S2 JOIN `Teams`  AS TM2 ON S2.`year` = TM2.`year` AND S2.`tmID` = TM2.`tmID` WHERE CAST(S2.`SHA` AS integer) >= 7);
original_question: Among the players whose short handed assists are greater or equal to 7, what is the final standing of the team with the most number of assists? Indicate the year to which the most number of assists was achieved and the name of the team.
original_evidence: short handed assists refers to SHA; SHA> = 7; final standing refers to rank; the final standing of the team with the most number of assists refers to max(A)
original_SQL: SELECT T2.rank, T2.year, T2.name FROM Scoring AS T1 INNER JOIN Teams AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.SHA >= 7 ORDER BY T1.A DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the teams that had more wins than loses in the year 2006, how many of them have over 100 points?
evidence: more wins than loses refers to W > L; over 100 points refers to Pts > 100
SQL: SELECT COUNT(tmID) FROM Teams WHERE year = 2006 AND W > L AND Pts > 100

question: Please list the names of the first 3 teams that got the most penalty minutes in 2006.
evidence: the first 3 teams that got the most penalty minutes refer to name where MAX(PIM) limit to 3; year = 2006;
SQL: SELECT name FROM Teams WHERE year = 2006 GROUP BY tmID, name ORDER BY SUM(PIM) DESC LIMIT 3
original_question: Please list the first 3 teams that got the most penalty minutes in 2006.

question: Please list the names of the teams that had a tie in 1909.
evidence: tie refers to T>0; year = 1909;
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT `name` FROM `Teams` WHERE `year` = 1909 AND `T` > 0
original_SQL: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM TeamVsTeam AS T1 INNER JOIN Teams AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Teams AS T3 ON T1.year = T3.year AND T1.oppID = T3.tmID WHERE T1.year = 1909 AND T1.T = 1

question: What is the total number of game played for players from USA?
evidence: game played refers to GP; from USA refers to birthCountry = 'USA';
SQL: SELECT SUM(`GP`) AS total_games FROM `Scoring` AS s JOIN `Master` AS m ON s.`playerID` = m.`playerID` WHERE m.`birthCountry` = 'USA'
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.GP) FROM Master AS T1 INNER JOIN Scoring AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.birthCountry = 'USA'

question: What are the life expectancies of residents in the most crowded cities?
evidence: most crowded city refers to MAX(Population);
SQL: SELECT T2.LifeExpectancy FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T1.Population = (SELECT MAX(Population) FROM City);
original_question: What is the life expectancy of residents in the most crowded city?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.LifeExpectancy FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code ORDER BY T1.Population DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which country has the most crowded city? If multiple countries has the same most crowded city, report the country that has the smallest surface area.
evidence: smallest surface area refers to MIN(smallest surface area); most crowded city refers to MAX(Population);
SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code ORDER BY T1.Population DESC, T2.SurfaceArea DESC LIMIT 1
original_question: Which country has the smallest surface area and the most crowded city?

question: What are the cities for country called "´Uman" in local name.
SQL: SELECT T1.Name FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T2.LocalName = '´Uman'
original_question: What are the cities for country called "Â´Uman" in local name.
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Name FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T2.LocalName = 'Â´Uman'

question: How many cities are there in the country with the surface area of 652090?
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.Name) FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T2.SurfaceArea = 652090 GROUP BY T2.Name
original_SQL: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Name) FROM City AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T2.SurfaceArea = 652090 GROUP BY T2.Name

question: Write the names of the podcasts in the music category that have a rating greater than 3.
evidence: music category refers to category = 'music'; rating greater than 3 refers to rating > 3; name of the podcast refers to title
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT p.title FROM `podcasts` AS p JOIN `categories` AS c ON c.podcast_id = p.podcast_id JOIN `reviews`    AS r ON r.podcast_id = p.podcast_id WHERE c.category = 'music' AND r.rating > 3
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.title FROM categories AS T1 INNER JOIN reviews AS T2 ON T2.podcast_id = T1.podcast_id WHERE T1.category = 'music' AND T2.rating > 3

question: What are the longest reviews?
evidence: review refers to content; longest review refers to Max(length(content))
SQL: SELECT `content` FROM `reviews` WHERE LENGTH(`content`) = (SELECT MAX(LENGTH(content)) FROM reviews)
original_question: What is the longest review?
original_evidence: review refers to content; longest review refers to Max(content)
original_SQL: SELECT title FROM reviews ORDER BY LENGTH(content) DESC LIMIT 1

question: In 2014, what are the names of the universities which were considered leaders in the publications rank?
evidence: In 2014 refers to year = 2014; leader refers to MAX(score); in the publications rank refers to criteria_name = 'Publications Rank'; name of university refers to university_name;
SQL: SELECT T3.university_name FROM ranking_criteria AS T1 INNER JOIN university_ranking_year AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.ranking_criteria_id INNER JOIN university AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.university_id WHERE T1.criteria_name = 'Publications Rank' AND T2.year = 2014 AND T1.id = 17 AND T2.score = (SELECT MAX(score) FROM university_ranking_year WHERE ranking_criteria_id = 17 AND year = 2014);
original_question: In 2014, what is the name of the university which was considered a leader in the publications rank?
original_SQL: SELECT T3.university_name FROM ranking_criteria AS T1 INNER JOIN university_ranking_year AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.ranking_criteria_id INNER JOIN university AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.university_id WHERE T1.criteria_name = 'Publications Rank' AND T2.year = 2014 AND T1.id = 17 ORDER BY T2.score DESC LIMIT 1

question: What is the percentage of Harvard university's international students in 2011?
evidence: Harvard university's refers to university_name = 'Harvard University'; in 2011 refers to year = 2011; percentage of Harvard university's international students refers to pct_international_students
SQL: SELECT T1.pct_international_students FROM university_year AS T1 INNER JOIN university AS T2 ON T1.university_id = T2.id WHERE T1.year = 2011 AND T2.university_name = 'Harvard University'

question: Provide the ranking criteria and scores in 2005 that were received by Harvard University.
evidence: Harvard University refers to university_name = 'Harvard University'; in 2005 refers to year = 2005; ranking criteria refers to criteria_name;
SQL: SELECT T1.criteria_name, T2.score FROM ranking_criteria AS T1 INNER JOIN university_ranking_year AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.ranking_criteria_id INNER JOIN university AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.university_id WHERE T3.university_name = 'Harvard University' AND T2.year = 2005

question: Please list the names of all the ranking criteria of Harvard University in 2011.
evidence: in 2011 refers to year 2011; Harvard University refers to university_name = 'Harvard University'; names of all the ranking criteria refers to criteria_name
SQL: SELECT T1.criteria_name FROM ranking_criteria AS T1 INNER JOIN university_ranking_year AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.ranking_criteria_id INNER JOIN university AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.university_id WHERE T3.university_name = 'Harvard University' AND T2.year = 2011

question: What is the total number of ranking criteria under the ranking system called Shanghai Ranking?
evidence: ranking system called Shanghai Ranking refers to system_name = 'Shanghai Ranking';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ranking_system AS T1 INNER JOIN ranking_criteria AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.ranking_system_id WHERE T1.system_name = 'Shanghai Ranking'

question: How many universities are located in Japan?
evidence: located in Japan refers to country_name = 'Japan';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM university AS T1 INNER JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.country_id = T2.id WHERE T2.country_name = 'Japan'

question: What was the temperature range of station no.1 on 2012/1/1?
evidence: on 2012/1/1 refers to date = '2012-01-01'; temperature range refers to Subtract (tmax, tmin); station no.1 refers to station_nbr = 1
SQL: SELECT tmax - tmin AS temrange FROM weather WHERE station_nbr = 1 AND `date` = '2012-01-01'

question: How many no.9 items from store no.11 were sold on 2012/12/7?
evidence: no. 9 item refers to item_nbr = 9; store no.11 refers to store_nbr = 11; sold on 2012/12/7 refers to date = '2012-12-07'
SQL: SELECT units FROM sales_in_weather WHERE `date` = '2012-12-07' AND store_nbr = 11 AND item_nbr = 9

question: How many units are being sold for item 1 when the average temperature is 83?
evidence: item 1 refers to item_nbr = 1; when the average temperature is 83 refers to tavg = 83
SQL: SELECT SUM(s.`units`) FROM `sales_in_weather` s JOIN `relation` r   ON s.`store_nbr` = r.`store_nbr` JOIN `weather`  w   ON w.`station_nbr` = r.`station_nbr` AND w.`date` = s.`date` WHERE s.`item_nbr` = 1 AND w.`tavg` = 83
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(units) FROM weather AS T1 INNER JOIN sales_in_weather AS T2 ON T1.`date` = T2.`date` INNER JOIN relation AS T3 ON T2.store_nbr = T3.store_nbr WHERE T2.item_nbr = 1 AND T1.tavg = 83

question: What is the average population growth rate of countries where more than 3 languages are used?
SQL: SELECT AVG(pg) FROM ( SELECT T3.Population_Growth pg FROM country AS T1  INNER JOIN language AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country  INNER JOIN population AS T3 ON T3.Country = T2.Country  WHERE T2.Country IN (SELECT Country FROM language GROUP BY Country HAVING COUNT(Country) > 3 ) GROUP BY T3.Country )
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T3.Population_Growth) / COUNT(T3.Country) FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN language AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country INNER JOIN population AS T3 ON T3.Country = T2.Country WHERE T2.Country IN ( SELECT Country FROM language GROUP BY Country HAVING COUNT(Country) > 3 ) GROUP BY T3.Country

question: In which province is city Glenrothes located? What is the capital of the province?
SQL: SELECT T2.Province, T1.Capital FROM province AS T1 INNER JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Name = T2.Province AND T1.Country = T2.Country WHERE T2.Name = 'Glenrothes'

question: Which are the rivers that flows to Black Sea?
evidence: Black Sea is a sea located in Eastern Europe and Western Asia
SQL: SELECT Name FROM river WHERE Sea = 'Black Sea'

question: What are the names of the sea that can be found on the island with the biggest area?
SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM islandIn AS T1 INNER JOIN sea AS T2 ON T2.Name = T1.Sea WHERE T1.Island = ( SELECT Name FROM island ORDER BY Area DESC LIMIT 1 )

question: Which religions have the majority of the people in Japan?
evidence: Japan is a country
SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN religion AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T1.Name = 'Japan' AND T2.Percentage = (SELECT MAX(Percentage) FROM religion AS T3 WHERE T3.Country = T1.Code);
original_question: Which religion has the majority of the people in Japan?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN religion AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T1.Name = 'Japan' ORDER BY T2.Percentage DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which river with its mouth in the Donau River and a length greater than 500 km is located in Slovenia?
SQL: SELECT T2.River FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN geo_river AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T1.Name = 'Slovenia' AND T2.River IN ( SELECT NAME FROM river WHERE Length > 500 AND River = 'Donau' )

question: In which city is the sea whose depth is 3969 meters less than that of the Bay of Bengal?
SQL: SELECT T2.City FROM sea AS T1 INNER JOIN located AS T2 ON T1.Name = T2.Sea INNER JOIN city AS T3 ON T3.Name = T2.City WHERE ( SELECT Depth FROM sea WHERE Name LIKE 'Bay of Bengal' ) - T1.Depth = 3969
original_question: In which city is the sea whose depth is 4232 meters less than that of the Bay of Bengal?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.City FROM sea AS T1 INNER JOIN located AS T2 ON T1.Name = T2.Sea INNER JOIN city AS T3 ON T3.Name = T2.City WHERE ( SELECT Depth FROM sea WHERE Name LIKE '%Bengal%' ) - T1.Depth = 4235

question: Of all the countries that share territory with more than one continent, in which of them does the average population not exceed 10 inhabitants per square kilometer?
SQL: SELECT NAME FROM country WHERE CODE IN ( SELECT country FROM encompasses GROUP BY country HAVING COUNT(continent) > 1 ) AND population / Area <= 10

question: Which United States provinces are homes to the greatest number of corporations' corporate headquarters?
evidence: Organization refers to corporation
SQL: SELECT `Province` FROM `organization` WHERE `Country` = 'USA' GROUP BY `Province` HAVING COUNT(Province) = (SELECT MAX(Counted) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Province) AS Counted FROM `organization` WHERE `Country` = 'USA' GROUP BY `Province`));
original_question: Which United States province is home to the greatest number of corporations' corporate headquarters?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Province FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN organization AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T1.Name = 'United States' GROUP BY T1.Province ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Name) DESC LIMIT 1

question: What sea does the Baltic Sea converge with, and how deep is the Baltic Sea?
evidence: Coverage refers to mergesWith
SQL: SELECT T2.Sea2, T1.Depth FROM sea AS T1 INNER JOIN mergesWith AS T2 ON T1.Name = T2.Sea1 WHERE T1.Name = 'Baltic Sea'

question: Among the organizations where headquarters are in the 'USA', what is the percentage of the them are in 'Washington'?
evidence: percentage can be computed by [count(City = 'Washington') / count(all cities)] * 100%
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.City = 'Washington' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.City) FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN organization AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T2.Country = 'USA'

question: List the full name of its capital of all the countries with parliamentary democracy government.
evidence: Parliamentary democracy is a government form
SQL: SELECT T1.Capital FROM country AS T1 INNER JOIN politics AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Country WHERE T2.Government = 'parliamentary democracy'
original_question: List the full name its capital of all the countries with parliamentary democracy government.

question: Please list the occupations of the customers over 40 and have sent a true response to the incentive mailing sent by the marketing department.
evidence: over 40 refers to age > 40; RESPONSE = 'true';
SQL: WITH AllMailings AS (SELECT * FROM Mailings1_2 UNION SELECT * FROM mailings3) SELECT DISTINCT T1.OCCUPATION FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN AllMailings AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.REFID WHERE T1.age > 40 AND T2.RESPONSE = 'true'
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.OCCUPATION FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Mailings1_2 AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.REFID WHERE T1.age > 40 AND T2.RESPONSE = 'true'

question: Of the all customers who sent a true response to the incentive mailing sent by the marketing department, how many of them are divorced males?
evidence: RESPONSE = 'true'; SEX = 'Male'; MARITAL_STATUS = 'Divorced';
SQL: WITH AllMailings AS (SELECT * FROM Mailings1_2 UNION SELECT * FROM mailings3) SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN AllMailings AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.REFID WHERE T1.SEX = 'Male' AND T1.MARITAL_STATUS = 'Divorced' AND T2.RESPONSE = 'true'
original_question: Of the first 60,000 customers who sent a true response to the incentive mailing sent by the marketing department, how many of them are divorced males?
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Mailings1_2 AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.REFID WHERE T1.SEX = 'Male' AND T1.MARITAL_STATUS = 'Divorced' AND T2.RESPONSE = 'true'

question: What is the age of female customers within the number of inhabitants below 30?
evidence: female customers within the number of inhabitants below 30 refer to SEX = 'Female' where INHABITANTS_K < 30;
SQL: SELECT age FROM Customers WHERE GEOID IN ( SELECT GEOID FROM Demog WHERE INHABITANTS_K < 30 ) AND SEX = 'Female'

question: List the income and number of inhabitants of customers with a reference ID greater than the 50% of the total number of false responses?
evidence: reference ID greater than the 50% of average of number of false response refers to REFID > DIVIDE(MULTIPLY(0.5, COUNT(RESPONSE = 'false')), COUNT(RESPONSE)); income refers to INCOME_K; number of inhabitants refer to INHABITANTS_K;
SQL: WITH AllMailings AS (SELECT * FROM Mailings1_2 UNION SELECT * FROM mailings3) SELECT d.`INCOME_K`, d.`INHABITANTS_K` FROM   Customers    c JOIN   Demog       d ON c.`GEOID` = d.`GEOID` JOIN   AllMailings m ON c.`ID`    = m.`REFID` WHERE  m.`REFID` > ( SELECT 0.5 * SUM(CASE WHEN `RESPONSE` = 'false' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM   AllMailings )
original_question: List the income and number of inhabitants of customers with a reference ID greater than the 50% of average of number of false response?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.INCOME_K, T2.INHABITANTS_K FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Demog AS T2 ON T1.GEOID = T2.GEOID INNER JOIN Mailings1_2 AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.REFID WHERE T3.REFID > ( SELECT 0.5 * COUNT(CASE WHEN RESPONSE = 'false' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) / COUNT(RESPONSE) FROM Mailings1_2 )

question: What is the percentage of under $500 thefts among all cases that happened in West Englewood?
evidence: under $500 refers to secondary_description = '$500 AND UNDER'; theft refers to primary_description = 'THEFT'; West Englewood refers to community_area_name = 'West Englewood'; percentage = divide(count(case_number where secondary_description = '$500 AND UNDER'), count(case_number)) where primary_description = 'THEFT' and community_area_name = 'West Englewood' * 100%
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN i.`primary_description` = 'THEFT' AND i.`secondary_description` = '$500 AND UNDER' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS pct_under_500_theft_all_cases FROM `Crime`            c JOIN `Community_Area`   ca ON c.`community_area_no` = ca.`community_area_no` JOIN `IUCR`             i  ON c.`iucr_no`          = i.`iucr_no` WHERE ca.`community_area_name` = 'West Englewood'
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.secondary_description = '$500 AND UNDER' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.case_number) FROM Crime AS T1 INNER JOIN IUCR AS T2 ON T1.iucr_no = T2.iucr_no INNER JOIN Community_Area AS T3 ON T1.community_area_no = T3.community_area_no WHERE T2.primary_description = 'THEFT' AND T3.community_area_name = 'West Englewood'

question: Provide the responsible person and his/her email address of Chicago Lawn.
evidence: responsible person refers to commander; email address refers to email; Chicago Lawn refers to district_name = 'Chicago Lawn'
SQL: SELECT commander, email FROM District WHERE district_name = 'Chicago Lawn'

question: Provide the occurrence date and location of the deceptive practice due to the unlawful use of recorded sound.
evidence: location refers to latitude, longitude; deceptive practice refers to primary_description = 'DECEPTIVE PRACTICE'; unlawful use of recorded sound refers to secondary_description = 'UNLAWFUL USE OF RECORDED SOUND'
SQL: SELECT T2.date, T2.latitude, T2.longitude FROM IUCR AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T2.iucr_no = T1.iucr_no WHERE T1.primary_description = 'DECEPTIVE PRACTICE' AND T1.secondary_description = 'UNLAWFUL USE OF RECORDED SOUND'

question: What is the district address associated with the case JB107731?
evidence: case JB107731 refers to case_number = 'JB107731'
SQL: SELECT T1.address FROM District AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T2.district_no = T1.district_no WHERE T2.case_number = 'JB107731'

question: Among the crimes, what percentage are severe?
evidence: severe refers to index_code = 'I'; percentage = divide(count(iucr_no where index_code = 'I'), count(iucr_no)) * 100%
SQL: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN i.`index_code` = 'I' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS severe_percentage FROM `Crime` c JOIN `IUCR` i ON c.`iucr_no` = i.`iucr_no`
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN index_code = 'I' THEN iucr_no ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(iucr_no) FROM IUCR

question: In which districts have there been the most intimidation-type crimes?
evidence: most intimidation-type crime refers to Max(Count(primary_description = 'INTIMIDATION')); district refers to district_name
SQL: SELECT T3.district_name FROM IUCR AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T2.iucr_no = T1.iucr_no INNER JOIN District AS T3 ON T3.district_no = T2.district_no WHERE T1.primary_description = 'INTIMIDATION' GROUP BY T3.district_name HAVING COUNT(T1.primary_description) = (SELECT MAX(Counted) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.primary_description) AS Counted FROM IUCR AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T2.iucr_no = T1.iucr_no INNER JOIN District AS T3 ON T3.district_no = T2.district_no WHERE T1.primary_description = 'INTIMIDATION' GROUP BY T3.district_name) AS counts);
original_question: In which district have there been more intimidation-type crimes?
original_evidence: more intimidation-type crime refers to Max(Count(primary_description = 'INTIMIDATION')); district refers to district_name
original_SQL: SELECT T3.district_name FROM IUCR AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T2.iucr_no = T1.iucr_no INNER JOIN District AS T3 ON T3.district_no = T2.district_no WHERE T1.primary_description = 'INTIMIDATION' GROUP BY T3.district_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.primary_description) DESC LIMIT 1

question: What is the general and specific description of incident 275?
evidence: incident 275 refers to iucr_no = 275; general description refers to primary_description; specific description refers to secondary_description
SQL: SELECT primary_description, secondary_description FROM IUCR WHERE iucr_no = 275

question: Please name three communities that experience the fraud incident.
evidence: communities refers to community_area_name; 'Fraud' is the title of crime
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.`community_area_name` FROM `FBI_Code` AS T1 JOIN `Crime` AS T2 ON T1.`fbi_code_no` = T2.`fbi_code_no` JOIN `Community_Area` AS T3 ON T2.`community_area_no` = T3.`community_area_no` WHERE T1.`title` = 'Fraud'
original_evidence: communities refers to community_area_name; 'Fraud Incident' is the title of crime
original_SQL: SELECT T3.community_area_name FROM FBI_Code AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.fbi_code_no = T2.fbi_code_no INNER JOIN Community_Area AS T3 ON T2.community_area_no = T3.community_area_no WHERE T1.title = 'Criminal Sexual Assault' LIMIT 3

question: What were the major types of crime that happened in the Rogers Park community area?
evidence: "Rogers Park" is the community_area_name; major type of crime refers to title
SQL: SELECT T1.`title` FROM `FBI_Code` AS T1 JOIN `Crime`  AS T2 ON T1.`fbi_code_no` = T2.`fbi_code_no` JOIN `Community_Area` AS T3 ON T2.`community_area_no` = T3.`community_area_no` WHERE T3.`community_area_name` = 'Rogers Park' GROUP BY T1.`title` HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(Counted) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS Counted FROM `FBI_Code` AS T1 JOIN `Crime`  AS T2 ON T1.`fbi_code_no` = T2.`fbi_code_no` JOIN `Community_Area` AS T3 ON T2.`community_area_no` = T3.`community_area_no` WHERE T3.`community_area_name` = 'Rogers Park' GROUP BY T1.`title`) AS counts);
original_question: What was the major type of crime that happened in the Rogers Park community area?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.fbi_code_no, T1.title FROM FBI_Code AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.fbi_code_no = T2.fbi_code_no INNER JOIN Community_Area AS T3 ON T2.community_area_no = T3.community_area_no WHERE T3.community_area_name = 'Rogers Park' GROUP BY T1.fbi_code_no, T1.title

question: Among the incidents in January, 2018, how many of them were stated "against Property" in the FBI classification?
evidence: in January 2018 refers to Substr(date, 1, 1) = '1' AND Substr(date, 5, 4) = '2018'; against property refers to crime_against = 'Property'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `Crime` AS c JOIN `FBI_Code` AS f ON f.`fbi_code_no` = c.`fbi_code_no` WHERE f.`crime_against` = 'Property' AND c.`date` LIKE '1/%/2018%'
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(T2.date, 5, 4) = '2018' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM FBI_Code AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.fbi_code_no = T2.fbi_code_no WHERE T1.crime_against = 'Property' AND SUBSTR(T2.date, 1, 1) = '1'

question: Among violations on 3rd June, 2014, describe any 5 names, located cities and tax codes of the eateries with high risk category.
evidence: eateries with high risk category refer to business_id where risk_category = 'High Risk'; 3rd June, 2014 refers to date = '2014-06-03';
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name, T2.city, T2.tax_code FROM violations AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T1.risk_category = 'High Risk' AND T1.`date` = '2014-06-03'
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name, T2.city, T2.tax_code FROM violations AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T1.risk_category = 'High Risk' AND T1.`date` = '2014-06-03' LIMIT 5

question: Who are the owner of the business that have a high risk violation of 103109 and are described as unclean or unsanitary food contact surfaces?
evidence: owner refers to owner_name; high risk violation of 103109 and described as unclean or unsanitary food contact surfaces refers to risk_category = 'High Risk' where violation_type_id = 103109 and description = 'Unclean or unsanitary food contact surfaces';
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.owner_name FROM violations AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T1.risk_category = 'High Risk' AND T1.violation_type_id = 103109 AND T1.description = 'Unclean or unsanitary food contact surfaces'
original_question: Who is the owner of the business that has a high risk violation of 103109 and described as unclean or unsanitary food contact surfaces?

question: How many recipes include the ingredient "graham cracker crumbs"?
evidence: 'graham cracker crumbs' is a name of an ingredient
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.`recipe_id`)   FROM `Ingredient` AS T1   JOIN `Quantity`  AS T2 ON T1.`ingredient_id` = T2.`ingredient_id`   WHERE T1.`name` = 'graham cracker crumbs'
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Ingredient AS T1 INNER JOIN Quantity AS T2 ON T1.ingredient_id = T2.ingredient_id WHERE T1.name = 'graham cracker crumbs'

question: How many ingredients are there in the recipes that are best in helping your body's natural defence against illness and infection?
evidence: best in helping your body's natural defence against illness and infection refers to MAX(vitamin_a);
SQL: WITH best_recipe AS ( SELECT recipe_id FROM Nutrition WHERE vitamin_a = (SELECT MAX(vitamin_a) FROM Nutrition) ) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Quantity WHERE recipe_id IN (SELECT recipe_id FROM best_recipe)
original_question: How many ingredients are there in the recipe that is best in helping your body's natural defence against illness and infection?
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Nutrition AS T1 INNER JOIN Quantity AS T2 ON T1.recipe_id = T2.recipe_id WHERE T1.vitamin_a > 0

question: How much is the minimum salary given to the position with the most complex work?
evidence: most complex work refers to MAX(educationrequired); minimum salary refers to minsalary
SQL: SELECT `minsalary` FROM `position` WHERE CAST(substr(`educationrequired`, 1, instr(`educationrequired`, ' ') - 1) AS INTEGER) = (SELECT MAX(CAST(substr(`educationrequired`, 1, instr(`educationrequired`, ' ') - 1) AS INTEGER)) FROM `position`)
original_SQL: SELECT minsalary FROM position ORDER BY educationrequired DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are the names of the stations that are less used by customers who borrow bikes from? Indicate when were the stations installed.
evidence: less used station where bikes are borrowed from refers to start_station_name which has the least number of customers; subscription_type = 'Customer'; when installed refers to installation_date;
SQL: SELECT T1.start_station_name, T2.installation_date FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN station AS T2 ON T2.name = T1.start_station_name WHERE T1.subscription_type = 'Customer' GROUP BY T1.start_station_name HAVING COUNT(T1.subscription_type) = (SELECT MIN(Counted) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.subscription_type) AS Counted FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN station AS T2 ON T2.name = T1.start_station_name WHERE T1.subscription_type = 'Customer' GROUP BY T1.start_station_name) AS counts);
original_question: What is the name of the station that is less used by customers who borrow bikes from? Indicate when was the station installed.
original_SQL: SELECT T1.start_station_name, T2.installation_date FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN station AS T2 ON T2.name = T1.start_station_name WHERE T1.subscription_type = 'Customer' GROUP BY T1.start_station_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.subscription_type) LIMIT 1

question: On 11/3/2013, which stations have no bike avaiable to borrow? Indicate the names of the stations.
evidence: time = '11/3/2013'; which stations are empty refers to bikes_available = '0';
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM station AS T1 INNER JOIN status AS T2 ON T2.station_id = T1.id WHERE T2.bikes_available = 0 AND T2.time LIKE '2013/11/03%'
original_question: On 11/3/2013, which stations are often empty? Indicate the names of the stations.

question: On 8/29/2013 at 6:14:01 PM, how many bikes were borrowed from San Jose Diridon Caltrain Station?
evidence: How many bikes borrowed can be computed as SUBTRACT(SUM(dock_count), bikes_available where name = 'San Jose Diridon Caltrain Station' and time = '2013/08/29 06:14:01');
SQL: SELECT station.dock_count - status.bikes_available AS borrowed FROM station JOIN status ON station.id = status.station_id WHERE station.name = 'San Jose Diridon Caltrain Station' AND status.time = '2013/08/29 18:14:01'
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T1.dock_count - T2.bikes_available) FROM station AS T1 INNER JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.name = 'San Jose Diridon Caltrain Station' AND T2.time = '2013/08/29 06:14:01'

question: Among users who created a list in the February of 2016 and were eligible for trial when they created the list, which user has the most number of followers in their list in February of 2016? Indicate their ids.
evidence: created a list in the February of 2016 refer to list_creation_date_utc BETWEEN 2/1/2016 and 2/29/2016; eligible for trial refers to user_eligible_for_trial = 1;
SQL:
WITH eligible_users AS (SELECT l.user_id AS user_id,
           l.list_followers AS list_followers
    FROM   `lists`       AS l
    JOIN   `lists_users` AS lu
           ON l.list_id = lu.list_id
          AND l.user_id = lu.user_id
    WHERE  lu.list_creation_date_utc BETWEEN '2016-02-01' AND '2016-02-29' AND user_eligible_for_trial = '1'
)
SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM eligible_users WHERE list_followers = (SELECT MAX(list_followers) FROM eligible_users)
original_question: How many users who created a list in the February of 2016 were eligible for trial when they created the list? Indicate the user id of the user who has the most number of followers in his list in February of 2016.
original_SQL: SELECT T1.list_followers FROM lists AS T1 INNER JOIN lists_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id AND T1.list_id = T2.list_id WHERE T2.list_creation_date_utc BETWEEN '2016-02-01' AND '2016-02-29' AND T2.user_eligible_for_trial = 1

question: For each director who have directed at least 10 movies between 1960 and 1985, among the movies directed by this director between 1960 and 1985, which movie received the highest amount of 5 rating score? List the movie titles and the amount of 5 rating scores they received.
evidence: directed at least 10 movies refers to count(direct_name)>=10; 1960 to 1985 refer to movie_release_year
SQL:
WITH qualifying_directors AS (
    SELECT `director_id`
    FROM `movies`
    WHERE `movie_release_year` BETWEEN 1960 AND 1985
    GROUP BY `director_id`
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
),
qualifying_movies AS ( 
    SELECT m.movie_id, m.director_id, m.movie_title
    FROM movies m
    JOIN qualifying_directors qd ON m.director_id = qd.director_id
    WHERE movie_release_year BETWEEN 1960 AND 1985
    GROUP BY m.movie_id
),
movie_five_counts AS ( 
    SELECT qm.movie_id, qm.director_id, qm.movie_title,
           SUM(CASE WHEN r.`rating_score` = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS five_count
    FROM qualifying_movies qm
    LEFT JOIN ratings r ON r.movie_id = qm.movie_id
    GROUP BY qm.movie_id
),
max_five_counts AS (
    SELECT `director_id`, MAX(`five_count`) AS max_five
    FROM movie_five_counts
    GROUP BY `director_id`
)
SELECT mvfc.movie_title, mxfc.max_five
FROM movie_five_counts mvfc
JOIN max_five_counts mxfc
ON mvfc.director_id = mxfc.director_id AND mvfc.five_count = mxfc.max_five
original_question: How many directors have directed atleast 10 movies between 1960 to 1985? Indicate the name of the movie in those years of each director that received the highest amount of 5 rating score.
original_evidence: directed at least 10 movies refers to count(direct_name)>10; 1960 to 1985 refer to movie_release_year
original_SQL: SELECT T2.director_name FROM ratings AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T2.movie_release_year BETWEEN 1960 AND 1985 GROUP BY T2.director_name HAVING COUNT(T2.movie_id) > 10

question: Who is the director that directed the highest number of movies in the 70s? If there are multiple directors with the same amount of movies, list all of their names and indicate the highest rating score that those movies got from the users.
evidence: highest number of movies COUNT(T1.movie_id); in the 70s refers to movie_release_year between 1970 and 1979
SQL:
WITH movies_70s AS (
  SELECT `movie_id`, `director_id`, `director_name`
  FROM `movies`
  WHERE `movie_release_year` BETWEEN 1970 AND 1979
),
counts AS (
  SELECT `director_id`, `director_name`, COUNT(*) AS num_movies
  FROM movies_70s
  GROUP BY `director_id`
),
max_cnt AS (
  SELECT MAX(num_movies) AS max_num FROM counts
),
 top_directors AS (
  SELECT c.`director_id`, c.`director_name`
  FROM counts c
  JOIN max_cnt m ON c.num_movies = m.max_num
)
SELECT td.`director_name`, MAX(r.`rating_score`) AS highest_rating
FROM top_directors td
JOIN movies_70s m ON m.`director_id` = td.`director_id`
LEFT JOIN ratings r ON r.`movie_id` = m.`movie_id`
GROUP BY td.`director_id`, td.`director_name`;
original_SQL: SELECT T2.director_name, T1.rating_score FROM ratings AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T2.movie_release_year BETWEEN 1970 AND 1979 GROUP BY T2.director_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.movie_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are the top 10 oldest movies and what are the average rating score for each movie? Indicate the ids of the movies, the average rating scores, the name of the director, and when the movies were released. For movies with the same release year, order by title alphabetically.
evidence: the average rating score refers to AVG(T2.rating_score); oldest movies refers to MIN(movie_release_year)
SQL:
SELECT
    m.`movie_id`,
    AVG(r.`rating_score`) AS avg_rating,
    m.`director_name`,
    m.`movie_release_year`
FROM `movies` AS m
LEFT JOIN `ratings` AS r
       ON r.`movie_id` = m.`movie_id`
WHERE m.movie_release_year IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY m.`movie_id`, m.`director_name`, m.`movie_release_year`
ORDER BY m.`movie_release_year` ASC,
         m.`movie_title` ASC
LIMIT 10;
original_question: What are the top 10 oldest movies and what are the average rating score for each movie? Indicate the name of the director and when the movies were released.
original_evidence: the average rating score refers to AVG(T2.rating_score); oldest movies refers to MIN(rating_timestamp_utc)
original_SQL: SELECT T2.movie_id, AVG(T1.rating_score), T2.director_name, T2.movie_release_year FROM ratings AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id ORDER BY T1.rating_timestamp_utc ASC LIMIT 10

question: List the name of the store with the highest quantity in sales? What is the least quantity title from the store's sale?
evidence: qty is abbreviation for quantity; highest quantity refers to MAX(qty); least quantity refers to MIN(qty)
SQL:
WITH top_store AS (
  SELECT `stor_id`
  FROM `sales`
  GROUP BY `stor_id`
  HAVING SUM(`qty`) = (
    SELECT SUM(qty) FROM sales GROUP BY stor_id ORDER BY SUM(qty) DESC LIMIT 1)
),
top_store_title_quantity AS (
  SELECT st.stor_name stor_name, st.stor_id stor_id, ti.title title, SUM(sa.`qty`) title_qty
  FROM   `sales`  sa
  JOIN   top_store ts ON sa.`stor_id` = ts.`stor_id`
  JOIN   `stores` st ON st.`stor_id` = sa.`stor_id`
  JOIN   `titles` ti ON ti.`title_id` = sa.`title_id`
  GROUP BY st.`stor_id`, ti.`title`
),
top_store_min_title_quantity AS (
  SELECT stor_id, MIN(title_qty) min_qty
  FROM top_store_title_quantity
  GROUP BY stor_id
)
SELECT tstq.`stor_name` stor_name, tstq.title
FROM   top_store_title_quantity tstq
JOIN   top_store_min_title_quantity tsmtq 
ON tstq.stor_id = tsmtq.stor_id AND tstq.title_qty = tsmtq.min_qty
original_question: Name the store with the highest quantity in sales? What is the least quantity title from the store's sale?
original_SQL: SELECT T3.stor_id, T2.title FROM sales AS T1 INNER JOIN titles AS T2 ON T1.title_id = T2.title_id INNER JOIN stores AS T3 ON T3.stor_id = T1.stor_id WHERE T3.stor_id = ( SELECT stor_id FROM sales GROUP BY stor_id ORDER BY SUM(qty) DESC LIMIT 1 ) GROUP BY T3.stor_id, T2.title ORDER BY SUM(T1.qty) ASC LIMIT 1

question: Name the publisher which has the most titles published in 1991.
evidence: most title published refers to MAX(count(title_id); published in 1991 refers to YEAR(pubdate) = 1991
SQL:
SELECT T2.pub_name 
FROM titles AS T1 
INNER JOIN publishers AS T2 ON T1.pub_id = T2.pub_id 
WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.pubdate) = '1991' 
GROUP BY T1.pub_id, T2.pub_name 
HAVING COUNT(T1.title_id) = (
  SELECT COUNT(T1.title_id) 
  FROM titles AS T1 
  INNER JOIN publishers AS T2 ON T1.pub_id = T2.pub_id 
  WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.pubdate) = '1991' 
  GROUP BY T1.pub_id, T2.pub_name 
  ORDER BY COUNT(T1.title_id) DESC 
  LIMIT 1)
original_SQL: SELECT T2.pub_name FROM titles AS T1 INNER JOIN publishers AS T2 ON T1.pub_id = T2.pub_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.pubdate) = '1991' GROUP BY T1.pub_id, T2.pub_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.title_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many days delay for the complaint call from Mr. Brantley Julian Stanley on 2012/5/18?
evidence: days delay for the complaint = SUBTRACT("date sent to company", "Date received"); Mr refers to sex = 'Male'; on 2012/5/18 refers to "Date received" = '2012-05-18';
SQL:
SELECT CAST( julianday(e.`Date sent to company`) -
             julianday(e.`Date received`) AS INT) AS days_delay
FROM   events  AS e
JOIN   client  AS c ON c.client_id = e.`Client_ID`
WHERE  e.`Date received` = '2012-05-18'
  AND  c.first  = 'Brantley'
  AND  c.middle = 'Julian'
  AND  c.last   = 'Stanley'
  AND  c.sex    = 'Male'
original_SQL: SELECT 365 * (strftime('%Y', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%Y', T2.`Date received`)) + 30 * (strftime('%M', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%M', T2.`Date received`)) + (strftime('%d', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%d', T2.`Date received`)) AS days FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T2.`Date received` = '2012-05-18' AND T1.sex = 'Male' AND T1.first = 'Brantley' AND T1.middle = 'Julian' AND T1.last = 'Stanley'

question: Among the clients born between 1980 and 2000, list the name of male clients who complained through referral.

SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first, T1.middle, T1.last FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T1.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2000 AND T1.sex = 'Male' AND T2.`Submitted via` = 'Referral'
original_SQL: SELECT T1.first, T1.middle, T1.last FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T1.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2000 AND T1.sex = 'Male' AND T2.`Submitted via` = 'Referral'

question: What is the medium through which most complaints are registered in Florida?
evidence: medium refers to "Submitted via"; most complaints refers to MAX(Complaint ID); Florida refers to state = 'florida';
SQL:
SELECT E.`Submitted via`
FROM `events` AS E
JOIN `client`  AS C ON E.`Client_ID` = C.`client_id`
WHERE C.`state` = 'FL'
GROUP BY E.`Submitted via`
HAVING COUNT(*) = (
  SELECT COUNT(*)
  FROM `events` AS E
  JOIN `client`  AS C ON E.`Client_ID` = C.`client_id`
  WHERE C.`state` = 'FL'
  GROUP BY E.`Submitted via`
  ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
)
original_SQL: SELECT T3.`Submitted via` FROM callcenterlogs AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.`rand client` = T2.client_id INNER JOIN events AS T3 ON T1.`Complaint ID` = T3.`Complaint ID` WHERE T2.state = 'FL' GROUP BY T1.`Complaint ID` ORDER BY COUNT(T1.`Complaint ID`) DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many complaints from female clients born in the year 2000 were not sent through the web?

SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.`Submitted via`) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T1.sex = 'Female' AND T1.year = 2000 AND T2.`Submitted via` != 'Web'

question: What percentage of clients who sent their complaints by postal mail are age 50 and older?
evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM("Submitted via" = 'Postal mail'), COUNT(client_id)), 1.0); sent their complaints by refers to "Submitted via"; age >= 50;
SQL:
WITH postal_clients AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c.client_id, c.age
    FROM events e
    JOIN client c ON c.client_id = e.Client_ID
    WHERE e.`Submitted via` = 'Postal mail'
)
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN age >= 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS pct_clients_50_plus
FROM postal_clients
original_evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM("Submitted via" = 'Postal mail'), COUNT(client_id)), 1.0); sent their complaints by refers to "Submitted via"; age > 50;
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.`Submitted via`) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T2.`Submitted via` = 'Postal mail'

question: How many clients who live in New York City submitted their complaints via fax?

original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T1.city = 'New York City' AND T2.`Submitted via` = 'Fax'

question: Among all the clients who complained about their credit cards, what is the percentage of male clients?
evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(sex = 'Male'), COUNT(client_id)), 1.0); male refers to sex = 'Male'; credit cards refers to Product = 'Credit card';
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN c.sex = 'Male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS pct_male
FROM (
  SELECT DISTINCT Client_ID
  FROM events
  WHERE Product = 'Credit card'
) cc
JOIN client c ON c.client_id = cc.Client_ID;
original_question: What is the percentage of male clients complaining about their credit cards?
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.sex = 'Male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.sex) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T2.Product = 'Credit card'

question: Compute the average time in minute for each age group
evidence: teenager refers to 13 < age < = 19; adult refers to 19 < age < = 65; elder refers to age > 65; average time per complaint = max(divide(sum(ser_time), count(ser_time)))
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 13 AND T1.age <= 19 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60.0 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 13 AND T1.age <= 19 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS teenagerAverageMins, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 19 AND T1.age <= 65 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60.0 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 19 AND T1.age <= 65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS adultAverageMins , CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 65 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60.0 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS elderAverageMins FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN callcenterlogs AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.`rand client`
original_evidence: teenager refers to 13 < age < = 19; adult refers to 19 < age < = 65; elder refers to age < = 65; highest average time per complaint = max(divide(sum(ser_time), count(ser_time)))
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 13 AND T1.age <= 19 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 13 AND T1.age <= 19 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS teenagerAverageMins, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 19 AND T1.age <= 65 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 19 AND T1.age <= 65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS adultAverageMins , CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 65 THEN 60 * strftime('%H', ser_time) + strftime('%M', ser_time) + strftime('%S', ser_time) / 60 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T1.age > 65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS elderAverageMins FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN callcenterlogs AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.`rand client`

question: Give me the full birthdate, email and phone number of the youngest client in Indianapolis .

SELECT T1.year, T1.month, T1.day, T1.email, T1.phone 
FROM client AS T1 
INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id 
WHERE T2.city = 'Indianapolis' 
AND T1.year || '-' || T1.month || '-' || T1.day = (
  SELECT T1.year || '-' || T1.month || '-' || T1.day 
  FROM client AS T1 
  INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id 
  WHERE T2.city = 'Indianapolis' 
  ORDER BY T1.year DESC, T1.month DESC, T1.day DESC LIMIT 1)

question: Among the movie in which Dariusz Wolski works as the director of photography, what is the percentage of those movie whose vote average is over 5.0?
evidence: director of photography refers to job = 'Director of Photography'; vote average is over 8.0 refers to vote_average > 5; percentage = divide(sum(movie_id) when vote_average > 5, count(movie_id)) as percentage
SQL:
SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN vote_average > 5 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(vote_average) 
FROM (
  SELECT T1.vote_average vote_average
  FROM movie AS T1 
  INNER JOIN movie_crew AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id 
  INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.person_id 
  WHERE T3.person_name = 'Dariusz Wolski' AND T2.job = 'Director of Photography'
  GROUP BY T1.movie_id)
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.vote_average > 5 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.vote_average) FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN movie_crew AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.person_id WHERE T3.person_name = 'Dariusz Wolski' AND T2.job = 'Director of Photography'

question: Calculate the 2016 gap between the average revenue for Indian and American films.
evidence: 2016 refers to release_date LIKE '2016%'; Indian and American films refers to country_name = 'India' and country_name = 'United States of America'; gap between the average revenue refers to subtract(divide(sum(revenue), count(movie_id)) when country_name = 'United States of America', divide(sum(revenue), count(movie_id)) when country_name = 'India')
SQL: SELECT AVG(CASE WHEN T3.COUNTry_name = 'United States of America' THEN T1.revenue END) - AVG(CASE WHEN T3.COUNTry_name = 'India' THEN T1.revenue END) AS CALCULATE FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN production_COUNTry AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN COUNTry AS T3 ON T2.COUNTry_id = T3.COUNTry_id WHERE CAST(STRFTIME('%Y', T1.release_date) AS INT) = 2016

question: Are there any post-production movies in Nederlands? 'YES' or 'NO'
evidence: post-production movies refers to movie_status = 'Post Production'; Nederlands refers to language_name = 'Nederlands';
SQL:
SELECT CASE
         WHEN EXISTS ( SELECT 1
                       FROM `movie` m
                       JOIN `movie_languages` ml ON m.`movie_id` = ml.`movie_id`
                       JOIN `language` l ON ml.`language_id` = l.`language_id`
                       WHERE m.`movie_status` = 'Post Production'
                         AND l.`language_name` = 'Nederlands')
         THEN 'YES'
         ELSE 'NO'
       END AS answer;
original_question: Are there any post-production movies in Nederlands?
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.movie_status = 'Post Production' THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END AS YORN FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN movie_languages AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN language AS T3 ON T2.language_id = T3.language_id WHERE T3.language_name = 'Nederlands'

question: How many movies have "vi" as their language code?

original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.movie_id) FROM movie_languages AS T1 INNER JOIN language AS T2 ON T1.language_id = T2.language_id WHERE T2.language_code = 'vi'

question: What percentage of films are made in the US?
evidence: films' and 'movies' are synonyms; made in the US refers to country_iso_code = 'US'; percentage = divide(sum(country_id) when country_iso_code = 'US', count(country_id)) * 100 as percentage
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 *
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.`country_iso_code` = 'US' THEN COALESCE(pc.`movie_id`, m.`movie_id`) END) /
       COUNT(DISTINCT COALESCE(pc.`movie_id`, m.`movie_id`))    AS us_movie_percentage
FROM `movie` AS m
FULL JOIN `production_country` pc ON m.`movie_id` = pc.`movie_id`
LEFT JOIN `country` c            ON pc.`country_id`  = c.`country_id`;
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T3.COUNTry_iso_code = 'US' THEN T1.movie_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.movie_id) FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN production_COUNTry AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN COUNTry AS T3 ON T2.COUNTry_id = T3.COUNTry_id

question: What is the average ratio between female and male characters in a movie?
evidence: female refers to gender = 'Female'; male refers to gender = 'Male'; average ratio = divide(sum(gender_id) when gender = 'Female', sum(gender_id) when gender = 'Male')
SQL:
SELECT AVG(1.0 * female_cnt / male_cnt) AS avg_female_male_ratio
FROM (
  SELECT mc.movie_id,
         SUM(CASE WHEN g.gender = 'Female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS female_cnt,
         SUM(CASE WHEN g.gender = 'Male'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS male_cnt
  FROM movie_cast mc
  JOIN gender g ON mc.gender_id = g.gender_id
  GROUP BY mc.movie_id
  HAVING male_cnt > 0 AND female_cnt > 0
) sub;
original_question: What is the average ratio between female and male actors in a movie?
original_evidence: female refers to gender = 'Female'；male refers to gender = 'Male'; average ratio = divide(divide(sum(gender_id) when gender = 'Female', sum(gender_id) when gender = 'Male'), count(movie_id)) as percentage
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.gender = 'Female' THEN T1.person_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.gender = 'Male' THEN T1.person_id ELSE NULL END) FROM movie_cast AS T1 INNER JOIN gender AS T2 ON T1.gender_id = T2.gender_id

question: Please list the IDs of all the trains that run in the east direction and have less than 4 cars.
evidence: less than 4 cars refers to COUNT(cars) < 4
SQL:
SELECT t.id
FROM trains AS t
LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
WHERE t.direction = 'east'
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) < 4;
original_evidence: less than 4 cars refers to carsNum < 4
original_SQL: SELECT T1.id FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, MAX(position) AS carsNum FROM cars GROUP BY train_id ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T1.direction = 'east' AND T2.carsNum < 4

question: In which direction does the train with an ellipse-shape car run?

SQL: SELECT T2.direction FROM cars AS T1 INNER JOIN trains AS T2 ON T1.train_id = T2.id WHERE T1.shape = 'ellipse'

question: Among the trains running west, how many trains have no more than one car with an open roof?
evidence: running west refers to direction = 'west'; open roof refers to roof = 'none'
SQL:
WITH one_car AS (
  SELECT t.id AS id
  FROM trains  AS t
  JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
  WHERE t.direction = 'west' AND c.roof = 'none'
  GROUP BY t.id
  HAVING COUNT(c.id) <= 1),
zero_car AS (
  SELECT t.id AS id
  FROM trains  AS t
  LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id AND c.roof = 'none'
  WHERE t.direction = 'west' AND c.train_id IS NULL
  GROUP BY t.id)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) FROM (SELECT id FROM one_car UNION SELECT id FROM zero_car);
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T1.direction = 'west' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)as count FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, COUNT(id) FROM cars WHERE roof = 'none' GROUP BY train_id HAVING COUNT(id) = 1 ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id

question: Provide the directions for all the trains that have 2 or less cars.
evidence: 2 or less cars refers to COUNT(cars) < = 2
SQL:
SELECT t.direction
FROM trains AS t
LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(c.id) <= 2;
original_evidence: 2 or less cars refers to trailPosi < = 2
original_SQL: SELECT T1.direction FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, MAX(position) AS trailPosi FROM cars GROUP BY train_id ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.trailPosi <= 2

question: In which Argentine city do users post the most tweets?
evidence: "Argentina" is the Country; post the most tweets refers to Max(Count(TweetID))
SQL:
SELECT T2.City 
FROM twitter AS T1 
INNER JOIN location AS T2 ON T2.LocationID = T1.LocationID 
WHERE T2.Country = 'Argentina' 
GROUP BY T2.City 
HAVING COUNT(T1.TweetID) = (
  SELECT COUNT(T3.TweetID)
  FROM twitter AS T3 
  INNER JOIN location AS T4 ON T4.LocationID = T3.LocationID 
  WHERE T4.Country = 'Argentina' 
  GROUP BY T4.City
  ORDER BY COUNT(T3.TweetID) DESC LIMIT 1)
original_question: Users in which city of Argentina post the most tweets?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.City FROM twitter AS T1 INNER JOIN location AS T2 ON T2.LocationID = T1.LocationID WHERE T2.Country = 'Argentina' GROUP BY T2.City ORDER BY COUNT(T1.TweetID) DESC LIMIT 1
